Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment And Request For Dismissal Of Action
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAIME T. SAN GABRIEL OF THE ZEE LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-626-588-1600, ASSIGNEE OF RECORD COLLECT ACCESS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. LISA J. ESPADA, TEL. NO. 1-415-504-6195 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JESSIE CASTLE-HARRIS' Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment And Request For Dismissal Of Action - Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default and Request Dismissal is GRANTED as to setting aside default and DENIED as to the dismissal. Due Process entitles a defendant to notice of a hearing that may deprive them of a proprietary interest. (Mullane v. Central Hanover B. & T. Co. (1950) 339 U.S. 306, 314-315.) A "rebuttable presumption" of valid service is created when a registered process server signs a proof of service. (Evid. Code 647.) Defendant submits a declaration that Plaintiff's process server used an invalid address, and a declaration that she never received notice of the debt collection action. Plaintiff's opposition is unpersuasive because it does not include a declaration from the process server, who did not include his first name or a valid address on the Proof of Service of Process. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
